prompt: Need a tech design for the order projections service. PRD: /Users/nhoze/.claude/skills/tech-lead/evals/prd-order-events-consumer.md. The team is leaning toward DynamoDB for the store but i'd like a real principal-level take on storage AND on the consumer guarantees (we keep getting bitten by out-of-order events). Write it as a markdown file in this dir.

assertions.text: A single TDD-*.md file exists in the outputs/ directory
Before drafting, the assistant explicitly acknowledges having read the PRD with a one-line summary
Document is at least 1500 words
Contains a 'Problem' / 'Context' / 'Background' section
Contains a 'Scope' section with both in-scope and out-of-scope items
Contains a 'High-level approach' / 'Overview' section
Contains a 'Detailed design' section with substantive content
Contains 'Alternatives considered' with at least 2 alternatives and rejection rationale for each
Contains an NFRs section with concrete targets (numbers/SLOs)
NFRs section visibly accounts for every NFR — either a target or explicit 'N/A — reason'
Contains a 'Risks' / 'Failure modes' section
Contains a 'Rollout' / 'Migration' section
Contains an 'Observability' / 'Operations' section
Contains 'Open questions' with at least one entry
No fabricated/invented URLs to internal systems
TDD is structured as an event-driven / streaming consumer design
Explicitly states delivery semantics and what makes effects idempotent
Discusses per-partition ordering and partition key choice (order_id)
Specifies a consumer lag SLO with a concrete number
Specifies a DLQ for bad transitions / poison messages with trigger and replay path
Specifies offset commit strategy
Takes an actual position on storage (DynamoDB vs Postgres vs Redis) with stated tradeoffs
Addresses Avro schema evolution under BACKWARDS compatibility
Addresses out-of-order event handling within partition
Designs back-fill / full-replay path
Designs the GET /orders/{id}/projection read endpoint with latency target
